column3: LNP

column1: Adjusts preference flows by geography. Positive values increase flow toward that party; negative values reduce it. Applied after ideology but before caps.

column3: LNP
Weakly blends in the completed (Markov) matrix to smooth sharp edges when AEC data is sparse. Set to 0 to disable smoothing entirely.
How much weight to give seat-specific AEC conditional flows vs ideological priors when estimating unknown preference mass.
How many seats it takes to reach that weight
Maximum share any party can receive in a final two-candidate (2CP) count, unless overridden by strong AEC evidence.
Maximum share any party can receive in a three-candidate (3CP) scenario to prevent implausible dominance.
Tighter cap applied when IND or OTH are involved, preventing unrealistic landslide flows from minor parties.
Strength of primary-vote adjustment when allocating preferences between ALP and LNP (bounded by PRIMARY_TILT_MAX).
Hard limit on how much primary-vote differences can move preference flows in either direction.
Weak adjustment for IND/OTH preferences based on GRN vs ON primaries (used instead of LNP primaries to avoid teal distortions).
Maximum size of the weak GRN–ON tilt applied to IND or OTH preference flows.
Extra boost for IND → GRN preference flows in inner-metro seats, reflecting teal–Green voter overlap.
Smaller IND → GRN boost applied outside inner-metro seats.
Minimum floor applied to all alive parties to prevent zero-probability outcomes and numerical instability.
Forces some preference mass to be allocated by priors when AEC data shows zero flows to still-alive parties, preventing “two-destination collapse”.
Controls how strongly small-sample empirical posteriors are shrunk toward the prior.
Smooths posterior scenarios across seat classes (e.g. Inner Metro vs Provincial).
SIPHON_STRENGTH
Maximum share a donor party can lose to siphoning
Party-specific siphon aggressiveness
Coverage level where AEC blending begins
Coverage level where AEC reaches full strength
Overall multiplier on AEC influence
Proportion of posterior mass allowed to enter when a party is alive but missing from AEC row
AEC anchor strength when ON is eliminated in a final ALP–LNP count
Hard minimum share given to missing alive parties when posterior parsing yields zero
Maximum allowed AEC anchor weight when the AEC row is missing any alive party
Baseline AEC weight applied when alive parties are missing, regardless of coverage
